article_title: Bylaw 14788 — Housing Agreement (6479 Selma Avenue) Bylaw 2025

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby housing agreement for 74 rental units at 6479 Selma Avenue approved
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has passed Bylaw 14788, which allows the city to sign a housing agreement with Selma Developments Ltd. for a new rental building at 6479 Selma Avenue. The agreement requires that some of the 74 units be set aside as 'inclusionary units' — affordable rental homes for households with low to moderate incomes. The developer must rent these units to eligible tenants (households whose income is at or below limits set by BC Housing) at rents no higher than the median market rent for the area. The agreement also sets a penalty of $100 per day (adjusted for inflation) if the developer does not follow the rules.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required — this bylaw applies to the developer, not to existing landlords or tenants.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
Bylaw No. 14788 
Page 1 of 26 
Housing Agreement (6479 Selma Ave. 
Rezoning Reference #16-33 Subdivision Reference #21-17) 
Market Rental and Required Inclusionary Units 
CITY OF BURNABY 
BYLAW NO. 14788 
A BYLAW to authorize the execution of a Housing Agreement 
for the non-market rental housing development at 
6479 Selma Avenue 
The Council of the City of Burnaby ENACTS as follows: 
1.
This Bylaw may be cited as BURNABY HOUSING AGREEMENT (6479
SELMA AVENUE) BYLAW 2025.
2.
The City is hereby authorized to enter into a housing agreement with Selma
Developments Ltd., substantially in the form set out in Schedule “A” (the “Housing Agreement”),
for the non-market rental housing development on lands legally described as:
PID: 031-715-834, Lot A District Lot 94 Group 1 New Westminster District Plan 
EPP119499 
3.
The Corporate Officer is hereby authorized and empowered to execute the
Housing Agreement on behalf of the City.
FIRST READING this 25th day of November, 2025 
SECOND READING this 25th day of November, 2025 
THIRD READING this 25th day of November, 2025 
RECONSIDERED AND ADOPTED this 9th day of December, 2025 
       MAYOR 
DEPUTY CORPORATE OFFICER 
Signed Original Bylaw on File. 

Bylaw No. 14788 
Page 2 of 26 
 
 
Housing Agreement (6479 Selma Ave. 
Rezoning Reference #16-33 Subdivision Reference #21-17) 
Market Rental and Required Inclusionary Units 
HOUSING AGREEMENT 
(Section 483 Local Government Act) 
THIS AGREEMENT is dated for reference ____________________, 2025 
BETWEEN: 
SELMA DEVELOPMENTS LTD. 
Incorporation No. BC1046074 
7429 Morley Dr 
Burnaby, BC  V5E 3X9 
(the “Owner”)  
AND: 
CITY OF BURNABY 
4949 Canada Way 
Burnaby, B.C.  V5G 1M2 
(the “City”) 
 
WHEREAS: 
A. 
Section 483 of the Local Government Act permits the City to enter into and, by legal 
notation on title, note on title to lands, housing agreements which may include, without 
limitation, conditions with respect to the form of tenure of housing units; 
B. 
The Owner is the registered owner of the Lands (as hereinafter defined) and is currently 
constructing the Development on the Lands;  
C. 
The Owner may subdivide the Lands by Air Space Plan to create the Rental Air Space 
Parcel containing all the Market Rental Units and the Required Inclusionary Units; and 
D. 
The Owner and the City wish to enter into this Agreement (as hereinafter defined) to 
provide the Required Inclusionary Units (as hereinafter defined) as affordable housing on 
the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement.  
NOW THEREFORE in consideration of ten dollars ($10.00) and other good and valuable 
consideration (the receipt and sufficiency of which are acknowledged by both parties), and in 
consideration of the promises exchanged below, the Owner and the City covenant and agree as 
follows: 

Bylaw No. 14788 
Page 3 of 26 
 
 
Housing Agreement (6479 Selma Ave. 
Rezoning Reference #16-33 Subdivision Reference #21-17) 
Market Rental and Required Inclusionary Units 
ARTICLE 1 
DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION 
1.1 
In this Agreement the following words have the following meanings: 
(a) 
“Agreement” means this agreement together with all schedules, attachments and 
priority agreements attached hereto; 
(b) 
“Air Space Plan” means an air space subdivision plan of the Lands, or any portion 
thereof, pursuant to the Land Title Act;  
(c) 
“BC Housing” means the British Columbia Housing Management Commission;  
(d) 
“Business Day” means a day which is not a Saturday, Sunday or statutory holiday 
(as defined in the Employment Standards Act (British Columbia)) in British 
Columbia;  
(e) 
“CD Plan” means the comprehensive development plan for the Lands entitled 
“Selma Apartments” prepared by Jordan Kutev Architect Inc. and filed with the 
City’s General Manager Planning and Development; 
(f) 
“City” and “City of Burnaby” means the City of Burnaby and is called the “City” 
when referring to the corporate entity and “City of Burnaby” when referring to the 
geographic location; 
(g) 
“CMHC” means the Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation; 
(h) 
“CMHC Market Median Rent” means the median residential apartment rent 
applicable to areas within the City of Burnaby (being Southeast Burnaby CMHC 
Market Rental survey zone in the case of the Required Inclusionary Units under 
this Agreement), based on rental market data collected by CMHC, and if such rental 
market data is no longer published by CMHC, then such other methodology 
established by the City at its discretion; 
(i) 
“Daily Amount” means $100.00 per day as of January 1, 2018, and adjusted 
annually on January 1st of each subsequent year by a percentage equal to the 
percentage of the increase in the Vancouver Headline CPI for the period from 
January 1 to December 31 of the preceding calendar year; 
(j) 
“Development” means the development on the Lands of a six-storey, multiple-
family rental apartment development with 74 units constructed or to be constructed 
on the Lands in accordance with the CD Plan; 
(k) 
“Effective Date” has the meaning ascribed to it in section 10.2; 

Bylaw No. 14788 
Page 4 of 26 
 
 
Housing Agreement (6479 Selma Ave. 
Rezoning Reference #16-33 Subdivision Reference #21-17) 
Market Rental and Required Inclusionary Units 
(l) 
“Eligible Tenant” means a Household with a gross household income that does 
not exceed the Housing Income Limits (HILs) for the applicable unit type (or such 
other income threshold approved by the City at its discretion), but excluding: (A) 
the Owner, any directors, officers, and employees of the Owner, as applicable, and 
their respective direct family members; and (B) if the Owner contracts a third party 
to manage and administer the Required Inclusionary Units pursuant to 
sections 4.1(d) or 4.1(e), such third party, any directors, officers and employees of 
such third party, as applicable, and their respective direct family members; 
(m) 
“Funding Agreement” means an agreement entered into or to be entered into 
between a Funding Provider and the Owner with respect to the management of the 
Required Inclusionary Units, as the same may be amended, amended and restated, 
supplemented or modified from time to time;  
(n) 
“Funding Provider” means BC Housing, CMHC or another organization 
providing funding with respect to the development or operation, or both, of the 
Required Inclusionary Units;  
(o) 
“Household” has the meaning ascribed to the term “family” in the Zoning Bylaw, 
and in the event such term is no longer defined in the Zoning Bylaw, the last 
effective definition of the term “family” will apply; 
(p) 
“Housing Covenant” means the agreements, covenants and charges granted by the 
Owner to the City (which includes covenants pursuant to section 219 of the Land 
Title Act) charging the Lands, registered under number CB_________________, as 
it may be amended or replaced from time to time; 
(q) 
“Housing Income Limit (HILs)” means the gross annual income limit for the HILs 
Lower Mainland planning area associated with the City of Burnaby, as derived from 
CMHC’s annual Rental Market Survey and published by BC Housing from time to 
time, and if such gross annual income limit is no longer published by BC Housing, 
then “Housing Income Limit (HILs)” means the last such gross annual income 
limit published by BC Housing adjusted annually, on January 1st of each subsequent 
year, by a percentage equal to the percentage of the increase in the Vancouver 
Headline CPI for the period January 1 to December 31 of the preceding calendar 
year;  
(r) 
“Interpretation Act” means the Interpretation Act, R.S.B.C. 1996, Chapter 238 
together with all amendments thereto and replacements thereof; 
(s) 
“Land Title Act” means the Land Title Act, R.S.B.C. 1996, Chapter 250 together 
with all amendments thereto and replacements thereof; 

Bylaw No. 14788 
Page 5 of 26 
 
 
Housing Agreement (6479 Selma Ave. 
Rezoning Reference #16-33 Subdivision Reference #21-17)
